name: omarchy_system_snapshots
content:
System snapshots 
We create snapshots automaticay on every Omarchy update, but shoud you want to
create your own, you can use omarchy-snapshot create.
To boot and restore a snapshot, you seect it from the Limine boot oader. If you're
currenty booting straight into the Omarchy decryption screen, you' need to seect Limine
as a boot option via the BIOS first).
From that screen, choose the snapshot you'd ike to boot into based on the date and
version. The version of Omarchy at the time of the snapshot can be seen at the bottom eft
corner.
When you arrive inside, a notification wi popup notifying you that you're in a bootabe
snapshot and if you cick it, wi start the restoration process. Aternativey, you can
utiize omarchy-snapshot restore.
System snapshots · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/101/system-snapshots
1 of 2 2/2/26, 1:28 AM

---

This wi restore your /root , but not your /home . So it works for reverting a broken
system update, but not for recovering ost persona fies.
This aso means that your ~/.config  directory is kept as-is. So if you're roing back to an
earier version of a ibrary or appication that stores configuration fies in a new format,
you' have to sort that out manuay.
Note: This feature is ony avaiabe on instaations using the Limine boot oader, which
has been the defaut since Omarchy 2.0. It's not avaiabe if you're on GRUB or systemd-
boot.
System snapshots · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/101/system-snapshots
2 of 2 2/2/26, 1:28 AM
